name: CVXOPT (Convex Optimization for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-3.0); M. Andersen, J. Dahl, L. Vandenberghe

performance.notes: C-based interior-point methods; fast for medium-scale convex problems

agent_readiness.notes: Low-level convex optimization solver. from cvxopt import matrix, solvers. P = matrix([[2.,0.],[0.,2.]]). q = matrix([3.,1.]). sol = solvers.qp(P, q, G, h). Solvers: LP, QP, SOCP, SDP, GP, conelp, cp. Dense/sparse matrix support (cvxopt.matrix, cvxopt.spmatrix). Interior-point methods. Used as backend for CVXPY. Direct API is verbose — CVXPY (discipline convex programming) provides friendlier interface. ML use: kernel SVM QP solver. Signal processing: SOCP problems.

gotchas: Very low-level API — almost always use CVXPY instead
Custom matrix type (not NumPy) requires format conversion
Documentation assumes mathematical background in convex optimization
best_when: Backend solver when CVXPY is too slow (direct solver API)
Kernel SVM and QP problems requiring direct interior-point control
When studying convex optimization algorithms directly
Environments where CVXPY dependency is too heavy
avoid_when: All standard cases — CVXPY provides 10x better ergonomics
summary: CVXOPT is a low-level Python convex optimization library with C-based interior-point solvers. solvers.qp(P, q, G, h) for QP. solvers.lp() for LP. solvers.socp() for SOCP. solvers.sdp() for SDP. Uses custom cvxopt.matrix (not NumPy arrays). GPL-3.0. Almost always use CVXPY instead — CVXOPT is used as CVXPY's backend. Direct use justified only when CVXPY overhead matters or for studying interior-point methods.
